observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor: 

Require the Municipal Accounting Office to submit the BRS within the prescribed period and adjust reconciling items such as stale checks;  

Direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to send notices to the payees concerned on the existence of stale checks; and

Evaluate the current manning composition of the Accounting Office and consider replacement of the personnel in charge of the preparation of BRS and other necessary possible additions to the manpower, if necessary.

We have recommended that Management:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to update the Taxpayer Index Cards and submit the Certified List of Taxpayers to the Municipal Accounting Office for recording of RPT/SET Receivables as well as its corresponding reciprocal accounts; and

Require the Municipal Accounting Office to discontinue the practice of force balancing RPT/SET Receivables and the corresponding reciprocal accounts, regularly reconcile accounting records with that of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and draw Journal Entry Vouchers to correct all erroneous entries in order to reconcile such accounts.

We have recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Accountant and the Accounting Office to strictly comply with the requirement/s on the submission of financial reports and transaction documents as provided in Item 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular 2009-006;

In coordination with the Human Resource/Personnel and Accounting Office, evaluate / assess the Accounting Office’s current manpower whether it is sufficient to cater to the demands of the Office, and based on the results of the assessment, make and/or suggest necessary adjustments in the delegation of tasks to concerned personnel or in the Office’s composition to address the delay in submission of reports, if necessary; and

If warranted, impose appropriate sanctions to the concerned officers and employees as provided in Sections 122, 127 and 128 of P. D. No. 1445, and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

In coordination with the Municipal Assessor’s Office, carefully scrutinize and update the certified list of RPT Tax Delinquencies and provide aging thereof for proper monitoring.

We recommended We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Planning and Development Council and the budget committee during the preparation of annual budget for 20% Development Fund to include only Programs/Projects/Activities which are feasible and could be accomplished within the target date of completion; and

Implement the development projects programmed during the year as embodied in the Annual Investment /Development plan and ensure optimum utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the benefit of the constituents of the Municipality.

We recommended the following:

Instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to submit the lacking supporting documents as enumerated in Annex G; and

That the Municipal Mayor together with the BAC and End-user to submit a written letter explaining such infraction against Section 305(a), 336 and 350 of Republic Act (RA) 7160 as well as Section 119 of PD 1445, the discrepancies of the submitted PO, PR, RFQ against the actual conduct of training and why such expenditures be not disallowed in audit.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Government: 

Submit sufficient legal basis upholding the payment of additional honoraria/ benefits/ compensation to punong barangays and written communication justifying the payment of such expenditures duly supported with approved budgetary appropriation; and

Demand from the punong barangays the submission of official receipts and liquidation reports, purchase orders/contracts and required supporting documents evidencing the disbursement of the funds received to the Municipal Accounting Office, if there is any.

We have recommended that management:

Submit a written justification why the above transactions be not disallowed in audit since splitting of contracts were already among the audit observations communicated during the CY 2019 Annual Audit Report;

Submit the Contract and its required supporting documents in relation to the procurement of supplies for journalism of Jasaan District with Check No. 3359488 dated February 12, 2020 totaling P295,325.88; and

Submit the lacking BAC resolution recommending the alternative mode of procurement duly approved by the HOPE.
observations.managementAction: Appealed already file Issued with ND No. 2022-017-101(2020) dated October 18, 2022 and NS Nos. 2022-139 to 142-101(2020) all dated October 18, 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management should:

Submit the lacking supporting documents as mentioned in Section 362 of COA Circular 91-368 and Section 9.1.3.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012;

Comply with the aforementioned documentary requirements in the succeeding Repairs and Maintenance of Machineries, Equipment, Transportation Equipment including the Property Ledger Cards; and 

Direct the Municipal Accountant together with the Property Officer to reconstruct and establish the Maintenance/Repair records of each Machineries, Equipment, Transportation Equipment including Property Ledger Cards of the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts of the Municipal Government.

We recommended that Management should require the immediate submission of the lacking supporting documents in compliance with PD 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001 and in order to facilitate the completion of audit of expenditures.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entry to recognize the corresponding accumulated depreciation of all PPE in accordance with PPSAS 17 to fairly present their balances in the financial statements, copy furnish COA of the entries made.

We recommended that:

Submit Delivery receipts and Invoices stamped “received” by the Agency.

Submit Certificate of Inspection and Acceptance with date indicated.

Submit an inventory report on the materials transferred or used in other projects.

Submit waste materials report on the disposal of unusable materials.

We recommended that management:

Require the Accounting personnel and the persons claiming the travel allowance to submit the lacking documents and refrain from granting excessive travel claims; and

Refund the excess claims of travel expenses in the absence of the certification of the head of the agency as required.

We recommended that management require the concerned officials to:

a.	Submit immediately the subject Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents. Thereafter, coordinate with the accountable officers, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Budget Officer and all concerned to submit promptly or timely the disbursement reports together with the paid vouchers, payrolls and its supporting documents; and 

b.	Suspend the payment of the salaries of the officials responsible for the non-submission of the subject reports until they shall have complied with the requirements.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management concurred to the following:
Require both the former OIC Municipal Accountant and incumbent Municipal Accountant to closely coordinate and investigate the noted deficiencies found in the CY 2024 FS, and thereafter, consider restating the same to provide more reliable and accurate financial information that will be useful for various users in their decision making; and
Install and implement a stricter internal control policy that will ensure proper turnover of documents and reports prior to the issuance of clearance to outgoing personnel.

Recommendations:
We recommended and it was agreed that Management:
Require both the former OIC Municipal Accountant and incumbent Municipal Accountant to closely coordinate and investigate the discrepancy between the PPE carrying amount presented in the SFP and PPE schedule as presented in the Notes to FS, and thereafter, consider restating the same to provide more reliable and accurate presentation of PPE accounts in the financial statements;
Require the Municipal Accounting Office to fast track the review, tracing, and reconciliation of the variances between the PPE amounts as appearing in the Trial balance and the PPE schedule, including the unaccounted beginning balances, and prepare an updated PPE schedule which include specific details of a particular property for proper identification and monitoring;
For the semi-expendables and other items improperly recognized as PPEs, instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify tangible items, with costs below the capitalization threshold and already issued to end-users but have remained in the PPE accounts, following the suggested entries in accordance with the illustrative entries outlined in Annex C of COA Circular No. 2024-06 dated March 14, 2024:
Prior period adjustment 				xxx
Accumulated Depreciation- PPE Account			xxx
PPE Account				xxx
Create and instruct the Inventory Committee to prioritize undertaking the procedures indicated in COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account.  Considering the bulk of its PPE accounts, it is further recommended that the management avail of the conduct of one-time cleansing per account basis; and
In coordination with the Human Resource Management Office, avoid frequent movement of personnel assigned in the Accounting Office to establish continuity and proper accountability of records and reports, and review and provide additional and enough permanent manpower to both the Accounting Office and GSO to handle the heavy responsibility of the proper recording and monitoring of all the PPE accounts.

Recommendations:
We recommended and it was agreed that Management:
Instruct both the former Municipal Accountant-OIC and current Municipal Accountant to closely coordinate and reconcile its records with those of the bank, and effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balance of the loans payable and CIP accounts;
Make a written representation with the Bank requesting a schedule of loan amortization for CY 2025 up to the maturity date to serve as basis in the proper classification and presentation of the current portion of the loans payable in the year-end financial statements; and
In coordination with the Human Resource Management Office, provide additional manpower with sufficient accounting and/or bookkeeping knowledge in the Accounting Office and avoid frequent movement of personnel to establish continuity and ensure proper recording of transactions and preparation of reports.

We recommended and Management concurred to review and consider providing additional manpower to the Accounting Office to give priority in reconciling the subject Accounts Payable, including gathering the source and/or supporting documents to be able to establish a complete schedule/listing thereof.  Thereafter, submit to the Sanggunian, upon recommendation of the LCE, the final listing of unaccounted payables for approval of its reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the Municipality.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
5.18.1 Direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to:
a. Submit the monthly reports of collections and/or disbursements and other required reports to the Municipal Accountant within the prescribed period; and
b. Send notices to the payees concerned on the existence of stale checks.
5.18.2 Require the Municipal Accounting Office to:
a. Submit the BRS within the prescribed period and ensure that reconciling items are identified properly and adjusted accordingly and promptly;
b. Exert effort to obtain documents to support the identified and long outstanding prior years’ reconciling items and make necessary adjustments accordingly; and
c.  Coordinate with the LGU Treasurer with regard to the reporting of stale checks for necessary adjustment, and immediately process the closing of bank accounts which are deemed dormant and unnecessary;
5.18.3 Evaluate the current manning composition of the Accounting and Treasury Office and other concerned operating units, and consider possible qualified additions to the manpower specifically the personnel in charge of the preparation of BRS as well as the submission of reports and documents necessary for the prompt preparation of the BRS, respectively;
5.18.4 Issue a memorandum to all concerned offices / operating units requiring prompt submission of reports and documents necessary in the preparation of the BRS and other related reports; and
5.18.5  Consider enrolling the rest of the bank accounts in online banking to facilitate easy and prompt access and monitoring of account transactions.

We recommended and it was agreed that Management require the Municipal Accounting Office to:
Fast track and prioritize the reconciliation of remittance and billing files to resolve outstanding discrepancies;
Coordinate with the HRMO in updating the service records and maintaining close coordination with the GSIS by promptly submitting the required documents; and
Review / assess the roles of personnel assigned in the retrieval of documents and reconciliation thereof, and consider assigning additional staff to expedite the process.

We recommended and Management is agreeable to the following:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to correct and make adjustments to the affected accounts, and thereafter, ensure proper recognition of accounts relative thereto; and
Assess the composition of workforce within the Accounting Unit, in coordination with the Human Resource Management Office, and ensure that movements of personnel will not adversely affect the outputs of the department/unit by requiring proper turn-over of accountabilities by the outgoing personnel as well as providing proper orientation, training and guidance to the incoming officer / employee.

17.11 We recommended and it was agreed that management adopt strict   measures to regulate the grant of cash advances in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 by requiring the Municipal Accountant to:
Strictly and regularly monitor and enforce the settlement of unliquidated cash advances within the reglementary period;
For retired and resigned employees, exhaust all possible remedies to enforce liquidation or collection thereof.  Issue demand letters to the identified individuals/employees to immediately liquidate their cash advances and if still unacted, cause the withholding of any amount due to erring personnel; and
Ensure that previous cash advance is fully liquidated before granting additional cash advance.

We recommended and Management is amenable to undertake the following:
Instruct the BAC to immediately submit the above contracts and other supporting documents, and ensure thereafter the submission of contracts and all other required attachments to COA for review and evaluation within the prescribed period; and
Ensure that vacated vital positions be immediately filled or an alternative officer be delegated to take over temporarily the post pending the filling of the position to avoid hampering operations.

We recommended and it was agreed that Management:
Require the Municipal Accounting Office to cause the immediate submission of the Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) and cancelled checks, and thereafter, ensure the submission thereof to the Audit Team within the prescribed period;
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO) to ensure the timely submission of reports and cancelled checks to the Municipal Accounting Office; and
Issue a written policy to ensure prompt submission of reports and supporting documents by the MTO and other concerned personnel and departments and impose possible sanctions on non-compliance therewith.

We recommended and it was agreed that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the requirement/s on the submission of financial reports and transaction documents to prevent suspension of salaries and other appropriation sanctions;
Issue a written policy to ensure prompt submission of reports and supporting documents by the MTO and other concerned personnel and departments, and impose appropriate sanctions on non-compliance therewith; and
Assess the composition of workforce within the Accounting Unit, in coordination with the Human Resource Management Office, consider delegating additional personnel, if necessary, and ensure that movements of personnel will not adversely affect the outputs of the concerned department/unit by requiring proper turn-over of accountabilities by the outgoing personnel as well as providing replacement for the vacated position and proper orientation, training and guidance for the incoming officer/ employee.

We have recommended that Management require the following:
 
Assess the current condition of the said equipment and implement necessary steps to make the said equipment operational particularly demanding from the supplier the delivery of all the lacking items and replacement of the items delivered which were not in accordance with the specifications in the contract; and

Justification from the Inspection Officer and Supply Officer with regards to the inspection and acceptance of the delivery that did not conform with the specifications.

We have recommended that Management:

Instruct the Disposal Committee to immediately submit the required document/s (Annex C) needed for the completion of the Technical Inspection by COA TAG, and thereafter, undertake the proper disposal procedures of Supplies and/or Properties following the procedures provided in COA Circular 92-386; and

As to the irregular and undocumented disposal of unserviceable properties with appraised value amounting to ₱132,800:

Submit justification/ explanation thereon;

Exert effort to determine the name and address of the buyer and secure the necessary documents to prove the pertinent transaction made therewith; and

Ensure thereafter that all government transactions including disposal of unserviceable properties follow the proper procedures pursuant to pertinent laws, rules and regulations, and are duly supported with complete documentation.

We have recommended that Management:

Submit a written explanation/ justification on the deficiencies noted in the first and second progress payments made for the Construction of Secondary Water Lines in Brgys. Aplaya-Solana-Luz Banzon and a Potable Water Supply System (Level III) in Brgys. Bobontugan to San Antonio; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor the processing of progress payments vis-à-vis both the SWA and the loan amount released by the bank to avoid overpayment.

We have recommended that Management:

Ensure that claims for Terminal Leave Benefits and other obligations covered with sufficient appropriation shall be paid in full within the pertinent calendar year;

Revisit the process of prioritization of expenses with sufficient appropriation; and

Require the immediate submission of the lacking supporting documents and disbursement vouchers for completion of audit of the said expenditures.

We have recommended that Management provide justification/ explanation on the unavailability of cash back up for the GAD activities included in the GAD Plan and Budget, and thereafter ensure that appropriations are duly supported with cash to ensure that GAD PPAs are implemented as planned to attain the GAD objectives.

We have recommended that Management instruct the BAC and TWG to:

Carefully and cautiously evaluate the legal, technical and financial documents submitted by bidders during the preliminary evaluation and post-qualification before recommending the award of the contract; and

Explain in writing why the BAC and TWG shall not be held liable for passing the contractor as eligible without thorough evaluation of the documents.

We have recommended that Management:

Require the current Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the former Municipal Accountant to initiate and fast track the most feasible reconstruction of SLs based on available records and source documents, and ensure thereafter the preparation / maintenance of SLs starting CY 2024; and

Consider adoption of a computerized Accounting System for faster and easier processing, recording and monitoring of transactions and accounts.

We have recommended that Management:

Assess the composition of the BAC Secretariat and consider replacement of the said BAC Secretariat Chairperson who resigned last February 2023 to take charge or assist in the submission of the required documents; and

Instruct the BAC to immediately submit the above contracts and other supporting documents and ensure thereafter the submission of contracts and all other required attachments to COA for review and evaluation within the prescribed period.

a.	Direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the previous personnel in-charge and make a detailed analysis and trace the causes of the noted unreconciled balances of the intra agency accounts, and thereafter, effect necessary adjustments thereon; and 
 
b.	Consider the installation of Accounting System for a more reliable and traceable recording of accounts.

We recommended that Management require the Accounting Unit to:
a.	Immediately submit to COA duplicate copies of the ORs for CYs 2020 to 2022, and thereafter ensure submission thereof as attachment to the RCDs; and
b.	Refrain from retaining the duplicate copies of the ORs though photocopies of which may be secured for reference purposes, if necessary.

a.	Fast track the preparation of SLs for all Revenue Collectors based on the reports and source documents submitted by the Treasurer’s Office, and ensure preparation and maintenance thereof thereafter; and

b.	Conduct regular reconciliation of GL and SL balances.

2.	The collectors / AOs to regularly reconcile their cashbooks / equivalent records with the SLs / accounting records at least quarterly as required in the Cash Exam Manual.

a.	Make necessary adjustments in the books for the total borrowing costs incurred in CY 2022 by debiting the appropriate asset account and crediting the Prior Period Adjustment account in the amount of P1,319,047.27; and

b.	Ensure that borrowing costs capitalized during the period are presented and disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

Direct the GAD focal person to ensure that GAD PPAs formulated and included in the GPB and subsequently undertaken and charged against the GAD budget are in line with the purpose for which GAD was created which, above all, should address gender issues in adherence to the requirements of PCW-NEDA-DILG JMC No. 2016-01.

a.	Seek prior approval from the local sanggunian for the utilization of appropriations for projects described in general terms as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2021-003 dated October 29, 2012.

b.	Submit required authority from the SB for the Municipal Mayor to enter into a contract in behalf of the LGU to implement the projects totaling ₽7,142,878.33.

c.	Instruct the Municipal Development Council include in details programs and projects in the Annual Investment Program (AIP).

Instruct the officials concerned to strictly follow the provisions of Section 5.1.5 of COA circular no. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 by submitting monthly Report on Sources and Utilizations of LDRRMF within the prescribed period to facilitate timely review of charges to and balances of LDRRMF specific appropriations.

d.	Submit Certificate of Inspection and Acceptance with date indicated.

e.	Submit an inventory report on the materials transferred or used in other projects.

f.	Submit waste materials report on the disposal of unusable materials.

a.	Meticulously and judiciously plan the procurement process and adhere strictly with the Revised IRR of RA 9184, particularly, Sections 48.2, 52.1 and 54 regarding the conduct of public bidding for the procurement of drugs, medicines and hospital supplies and resort to alternative modes of procurement only in exceptional cases.

b.	Submit immediately the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers.

Require the proponents to submit the lacking supporting documents in order to prove the validity and propriety of the disbursements and to avoid suspensions and disallowances and

Should not grant subsequent educational researches without observing the guidelines set forth in the Department Order No. 16, s. 2017 dated March 20, 2017.

We recommended that management:

1.	Adopt the guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 specially in creating a working and competent Inventory Committee that will be able to complete the conduct of physical inventory in three months or less;

2.	Require the Accounting office to establish and update its PPE records and PPE accounts after the complete physical count is conducted; and

3. Require the Property Custodian to conduct and submit a complete RPCPPE perpetually in order to reconcile its records with the accounting office.

We recommended that management should:

Require the GSO/Chairman of the Inventory Committee, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to prepare and maintain an Inventory of all local roads and a road map alongside the preparation of the Annual report of Local Road Network and

direct the Municipal Accountant to establish the necessary records and provide adjusting journal entries to recognize the changes and depreciation expenses/impairment losses upon completion of the required inventory reports in compliance to COA Circular 2015-008 for the proper recognition and subsequent measurement of local roads and its components.

We recommended that management:

Strictly adheres to the provisions and declared policy of the State to conduct its procurement process to promote good governance and its effort to adhere to the principles of transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency, and economy; and

Plan properly its procurement activities to ensure that an open and competitive bidding is availed particularly in the procurement of catering services.

We have recommended the following:

a) Require the proponents to submit the lacking supporting documents in order to prove the validity and propriety of the disbursements and to avoid suspensions and disallowances and

Should not grant subsequent educational researches without observing the guidelines set forth in the Department Order No. 16, s. 2017 dated March 20, 2017.

We recommended that management:
Direct the municipal treasurer to send a written notice to the concerned payees at least one month before a check becomes stale; and
Direct the municipal accountant to make necessary adjustments in the books for the stale checks in accordance with Section 59 of the NGAS Manual Vol. I and Section 104 of the GAM for LGUs – Vol. I.

We recommended the management to:
a) Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment and require the submission of the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) giving priority to its Lands and, if possible, provide additional manpower to its inventory taking process in order to assure its completion and submission;
b) Direct the Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to establish its own Subsidiary Ledgers and corresponding Property Ledger Cards for each parcel of land and further reconcile the PPE balances once the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) is completed and submitted in compliance with Section 120 - Vol. 1 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and
c) Exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership over all parcel of lands through Transfer Certificate of Titles (TCT) in the name of the Municipality and to ensure that the LGU has legal rights over all recognized assets in its financial statements.

We recommended that Management should:
a) 	Strictly comply with DBM LBC No. 103 dated May 15, 2013 in granting RATA to its OICs and ensuring compliance with budgetary and documentary requirements; and
b) 	Strictly comply with the guidelines for designation as required in the revised Omnibus Rules on Appointments and other Human Resource Actions (ORAOHRA) including the approval and budgetary allocation of its plantilla positions from the local sanggunian.

We recommended the management to:
a) 	Cease further payments of monthly honoraria to national government officials until the Sangguniang Bayan of Jasaan pass a resolution authorizing the grant and specifying the rates of additional allowances to these officials, subject to availability of funds;
b) 	Strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth under Compensation Policy Guidelines No. 98-1 dated March 23, 1988 in granting allowances to BIR and COMELEC officers; and
c) 	Submit legal basis authorizing the payment of honorarium to the BIR, COMELEC and PNP-Provincial Security Guard Officials.

We recommended the management to:
Instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to closely coordinate with the Local Chief Executive and other members of the Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) for the implementation of development projects under the LGU’s investment plan to ensure that these plans are carried out on a timely manner and its purpose be achieved to the benefit of its constituents; and
Instruct the Municipal Engineering Office to verify the accuracy of the information on its quarterly report on government Projects/ Programs/ Activities before its submission.

We recommended that Management complies strictly the provisions provided by aforementioned laws and secure the required Environmental Compliance Certificate to avoid further penal and administrative liabilities.
We further recommended the Management to effectively and efficiently observe the implementation of the 10-year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan.

provide the Audit Team with sufficient legal basis to justify the above payrolls/disbursements.

Submit the legal basis authorizing such grant / payment of the said bonus.
Strictly adhere to the provisions cited under DBM Budget Circular No. 2016-4, dated April 28, 2016 and require the consultants and job order employees to refund the corresponding amount received.

We recommended that the Local Development Council should optimize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund through:
Proper planning of projects including timing and execution;
Coordinated action from LGU officials for optimum result; and
Start monitoring on project implementation to ensure accomplishment of the approved 20% Annual Investment Plan (AIP).

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant and the Human Resource Management Officer to submit immediately the required supporting documents to support the payment made pursuant to Section 4(6) of P.D 1445.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that for evaluation and monitoring purposes, management direct officials concerned to prepare and submit the Local Disaster Risk reduction and Management Fund Investment (LDRRMFP) as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 indicating the 30% allocation of QRF and the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness showing the projects and activities to be funded.

Likewise, we recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report on Source and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format in COA Circular No. 2012-002 and require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to submit such report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the COA Auditor.

We recommended that management comply strictly with the provision cited in Section 4 (3) of PD 1445. Trust fund shall not be used to pay for obligations not within the purpose for which the fund was created. The practice of borrowing funds from the trust fund to pay for obligations should be stopped immediately. We further recommended the immediate return of the full amount used to the Trust Fund.

We recommended that BAC Secretariat submit immediately the lacking bidding and contract documents in order to facilitate the contract and technical review as well as the technical inspection/evaluation of the projects.

We also recommended that the Municipality must strictly comply with the procedural requirements prescribed under Republic Act No. 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should observe the proper submission of accounts within the prescribed period as required under COA Circular No. 2009-006 and PD 1445 for proper and timely evaluation of the transactions.

We recommended that management comply strictly to the provisions of RA 9184 adopting public bidding as the general mode of procurement. Alternative methods shall be resorted only to highly exceptional cases as provided for in Rule XVI of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. Agency should stop the practice of procuring supplies and materials on reimbursement basis.

We further recommended compliance to sections 93 of PD 1445 requiring direct payment of government transactions to suppliers/creditors or to a Disbursing Officers

We recommended that Management strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and republic Act no. 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations and submit immediately the aforementioned lacking supporting documents. Further, the Municipal Treasurer should pay only claims that are duly approved/certified and supported with complete documentation.

We also recommended that the agency should desist from making payment of goods/equipment not yet delivered.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee should strictly observe the provision of Section 47 of RA 9184 which disqualified bidders having conflict of interest to participate in the procurement to avoid suspension and disallowance of the transaction.

We further recommended that BAC should submit a written explanation on why the contract was awarded to WGG Concreting Products and Mini Hardware.

We recommended that the Municipality fully comply and adhere strictly to the provisions of RA 7192 regarding the allocation of 5% of the total budget appropriation to implement GAD plan and Gender Responsive Program of the government
